display_name: Cost cadence / dry-dock / structural vs timing
description: Analysts press on the intra-year cadence of NCC ex-fuel, separating one-time dry-dock and crew-travel passthroughs from structural run-rate, and ask how this shapes FY normalization into 2027.

2022-Q2: NCC pacing into 2023 and whether inflation offsets ramp-down of one-time costs
2023-Q3: dry-dock as non-recurring carve-out inside the 300bp cost step for next year
2024-Q4: whether 2025 NCC embeds new destination (Nassau/RBC) costs and how it compares to CocoCay
2025-Q1: capacity-growth tweak tied to dry-dock scheduling vs delivery timing
2025-Q3: 'anemic' NCC growth: like-for-like vs inclusive of new destinations
port fees, dry-dock, Haiti as cadence callouts
2025-Q4: Q2 dry-dock concentration shaping yield/cost cadence
second straight year of unusually low NCC -- structural or harvested?
2026-Q1: capacity-growth threshold above which NCC ex-fuel reverts to 2-3% inflation
itinerary changes and deferred initiatives that flatter 2026 cost

analyst_affinity.robin farley: Asks for an explicit carve-out of dry-dock bps from the FY cost step and whether 'anemic' NCC is pre- or post-new-destination opening.
analyst_affinity.lizzie dove: Probes intra-year cost cadence with Q2 dry-dock concentration as the pivot point.
analyst_affinity.vince ciepiel: Lists port fees, dry-dock and Haiti as cadence items and asks whether they normalize in the out-year.
analyst_affinity.ben chaiken: Tests whether new owned-destination opex (Nassau, RBC) is embedded in NCC and benchmarks vs CocoCay efficiency.
analyst_affinity.andrew didora: Asks at what capacity-growth level NCC ex-fuel reverts to inflationary 2-3% range.
analyst_affinity.sharon zackfia: Frames lower NCC as either efficiency harvest or deferral of initiatives into 2027-2028.
analyst_affinity.daniel politzer: Asks for pacing of NCC in H2 and into next year with inflation overlay.

default_framing_options: structural vs timing carve-out within NCC ex-fuel growth
Q2 dry-dock concentration and its effect on intra-year cost cadence
FY run-rate normalization into 2027 once new-destination opex is fully loaded
